message: I am looking to sell at a price that ensures a fair profit for both of us. Let's aim for a mutually beneficial transaction.
My valuation is extremely low. I can only afford to trade around 10-12. If your cost allows it, let's aim for a price in that range so we can both make a profit across the 20 rounds.
Glad we could trade. Given my costs, I would like to aim for a slightly higher price point in future rounds to ensure sustainable trading for both of us. What do you think?
I genuinely can't go higher; my absolute cap is 13. If we increase the price, I'll be forced to stop trading. Let's stick to your ask of 10 and my bid of 12 to guarantee continuous mutual
I literally cannot go higher than 12. If you ask 13 or more, the trade will fail and we both get zero. Let's lock in 11 or 12 for the rest of the rounds. Please don't risk it!
I understand your constraints. Let's maintain a trade price of 11 or 12 for the remaining rounds to ensure we both consistently profit.
I agree to keep our ask and bid at 11 and 12, respectively, for the remaining rounds to ensure we both consistently profit from our transactions.

Last round the price rounded up to 12. To avoid unpredictable rounding, let's both submit exactly 11 from now on. I will bid 11, please ask 11. That guarantees an exact 11 clear every time.
